_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here is what was implemented and how it fits your lesson.

### 1. `App.jsx`
- **NEW import:** `ProductDetail`.
- **NEW route:** `/product/:id` → `ProductDetail` with `onAdd={addToCart}` so the cart still uses lifted state.
- Comments explain that `:id` is the dynamic segment of the URL.

### 2. `pages/ProductDetail.jsx`
- Uses **`useParams()`** for `id` and **`getProductById`** from shared data.
- Renders **name, price, description, ingredients list**, and **Add to Cart** with `onClick={() => onAdd()}` so the event object is not passed into `addToCart`.
- Comments mark imports and the move from an in-file array to shared data.

### 3. `data/products.js` (new)
- Single catalog for **Orange (1), Pineapple (2), Papaya (3)** so it matches your **Home** grid (your snippet used Apple/Orange/Papaya; the shop UI uses Orange/Pineapple/Papaya).
- Exports **`PRODUCTS`** and **`getProductById`** for the “search party” pattern.

### 4. `components/ProductCard.jsx`
- **NEW prop:** `id` (needed for `/product/${id}`).
- **`Link`** wraps the **`<img>`**; the old standalone `<img>` is left as a **comment** for class discussion.
- **`Link`** import and **`id`** in props are commented for teaching.

### 5. `pages/Home.jsx` + `Home.css`
- Your real catalog lives in **`products-row`**, not in `ProductCard`, so each **mascot `juice-image`** is wrapped in **`Link`** to **`/product/1`**, **`/product/2`**, **`/product/3`** (with short comments).
- **CSS:** `.juice-visual .juice-detail-link` positions the link; **responsive** sizes target `.juice-visual .juice-detail-link .juice-image`.

### Teaching note
When you reuse **`ProductCard`**, pass **`id`** (and the rest) like:

```jsx
<ProductCard id={1} name="Orange Juice" price="$3.20" img={…} onAdd={…} />
```

Invalid URLs (e.g. `/product/99`) show **“Product not found!”**.

`npm run build` completes successfully. If you want the catalog text to match the **Apple / Orange / Papaya** snippet exactly, we can swap the three rows in `data/products.js` and adjust the Home links to match.

---

_**User**_

great, now I want you to provide content that I show to students about whatever is performed. Use basic english. so that a 10 year old kid understands it. 

also mention which files and what you have changed. 

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here is wording you can show students, plus a **file list** of what was changed.

---

## For your students (simple English)

### The problem we solved
On the shop page you could see juices and add them to the cart. But each juice did not have its **own page** with a long story (description, ingredients, price in one place). We fixed that.

### Think of it like library books
- Each juice is like a **book**.
- Every book needs a **shelf number** so you can find it. For us, that number is the **id**: `1`, `2`, or `3`.
- The address in the browser looks like: **`/product/1`** or **`/product/2`**. The last part is **which juice** you picked.
- **`useParams`** is like reading that number from the address bar and saying: *“Oh, they want juice number 2.”*

### One list for the whole app
We keep all juice facts (name, price, story, ingredients) in **one shared list** (`data/products.js`).  
That way we do not copy-paste the same information in many places. **One truth, many screens.**

### How you open a juice page
- On the home page, when you **click the big fruit picture**, you “walk” to that juice’s page. That is a **`Link`** (a safe in-app link; the whole page does not blink and reload like old websites).
- **`ProductCard`** does the same idea: the **image is wrapped in a `Link`**, so a tap on the picture goes to **`/product/` + that id**.

### What the new page shows
**`ProductDetail`** is the “big card” for one juice: title, price, short story, ingredient list, and **Add to cart** (still using the same cart in `App`).

### If someone types a bad link
If the id does not match any juice (for example `/product/999`), we show **“Product not found!”** so the app does not break.

---

## Files we added or changed (what and why)

| File | What we did |
|------|----------------|
| **`src/data/products.js`** | **NEW.** The list of all juices (id, name, price, description, ingredients) plus a small helper to find one juice by id. |
| **`src/pages/ProductDetail.jsx`** | **NEW.** Page for one juice: reads `id` from the URL, finds that juice in the list, shows details and **Add to cart**. |
| **`src/App.jsx`** | **CHANGED.** Registered a **dynamic route** `/product/:id` and render `ProductDetail`, passing the same `addToCart` function as the home page so the cart stays one shared cart. |
| **`src/components/ProductCard.jsx`** | **CHANGED.** Added prop **`id`**. Wrapped the product **image** in **`<Link to={/product/${id}}>`** so the image opens the right detail page. Left the old plain `<img>` as a **comment** so you can compare before/after. |
| **`src/pages/Home.jsx`** | **CHANGED.** Imported **`Link`**. Wrapped each **mascot** image in the three juice cards with **`Link`** to `/product/1`, `/product/2`, `/product/3` so the real shop grid also opens detail pages. |
| **`src/Home.css`** | **CHANGED.** Styled **`.juice-detail-link`** so the linked picture still **sits in the same place** and does not get an ugly underline; updated the **mobile** sizes for the image inside that link. |

---

You can read the “For your students” block aloud or put it on a slide; the table is handy as a **checklist** (“open these files after class and match them to what we said”).
